commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR remaps `clawdock-dashboard` URLs to the Docker Compose published gateway port, appends/redacts dashboard token fragments for browser launch, and adds a changelog entry.

PR surface: Docs +1, Other +178. Total +179 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source. Current main and v2026.6.6 open the dashboard URL emitted inside the CLI container while Docker Compose can publish the gateway on a different host port.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can preserve the contributor's useful branch by resolving the conflict, removing the changelog edit, and adding the missing helper regression without a product decision.

target.title: fix(clawdock): open dashboard via clawdock-dashboard on published port instead of hardcoded default port
target.body:
## Summary

  - Problem: `clawdock-dashboard` could open the container-internal dashboard URL/port instead of the Docker-published host port.
  - Why it matters: Docker users with remapped ports could get a dashboard URL that does not work from the host browser, even though the gateway is healthy.
  - What changed: The helper now maps loopback dashboard URLs to the published Docker Compose gateway port, preserves scheme/path, appends the resolved auth token for browser launch, redacts the token in terminal output, and avoids fragile external command dependencies on the dashboard path.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No gateway runtime, dashboard UI, Docker Compose service, plugin, or API behavior changed.
  - Fixes #77344

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/77344
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: `openclaw dashboard --no-open` runs inside the Docker Compose network namespace and reports the gateway’s internal loopback URL/port. `clawdock-dashboard` reused that URL directly instead of translating it to the host-published Compose port.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: No ClawDock-specific smoke test covered Docker host-port remapping, token redaction, or fresh zsh shell compatibility for the shell helper.
  - Contributing context (if known): The issue appears when `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT` differs from the internal gateway port `18789`.


  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [ ] Unit test
    - [x] Seam / integration test
    - [ ] End-to-end test
    - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: A future ClawDock helper shell smoke test for `clawdock-dashboard`.
  - Scenario the test should lock in: Given Docker Compose publishes `openclaw-gateway` on a host port, `clawdock-dashboard` opens that
  published host URL, preserves scheme/path, includes the auth token only in the browser URL, and redacts it from terminal output.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior depends on shell helper parsing plus Docker Compose port output, not gateway
  internals.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None found for `scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh`.
  - If no new test is added, why not: This PR keeps the change scoped to the existing standalone shell helper, which does not currently have a
  test harness.

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  `clawdock-dashboard` now opens the host-accessible Docker-published dashboard URL, such as `http://127.0.0.1:3200/`, instead of the
  container-internal `18789` URL. Tokenized URLs are still used for browser auth, but terminal output shows `#token=<redacted>`.

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```text
  Before:
  clawdock-dashboard -> openclaw-cli inside Docker -> http://127.0.0.1:18789/ -> host browser may fail

  After:
  clawdock-dashboard -> openclaw-cli inside Docker -> remap to Compose published port -> host browser opens dashboard
  ```

  ## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (No)
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes)
  - New/changed network calls? (No)
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
  - Data access scope changed? (No)
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: The helper now reconstructs the browser URL with an auth token when the CLI indicates token auto-
    auth is available. The token is sent only to the browser/open command and is redacted from terminal output and fallback instructions.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Docker Compose, openclaw-gateway published on host port 3200
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): ClawDock dashboard helper
  - Relevant config (redacted): OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<redacted>, gateway internal port 18789, published host port 3200

  ### Steps

  1. Start the OpenClaw Docker Compose gateway with a host-published gateway port different from 18789.
  2. Source scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh.
  3. Run clawdock-dashboard.

  ### Expected

  - The helper opens the host-published dashboard URL.
  - The browser/open command receives the tokenized URL.
  - Terminal output redacts the token.

  ### Actual

  - Before: the helper used the CLI-reported internal dashboard URL.
  - After: the helper opens http://127.0.0.1:3200/#token=<redacted> in the local Docker smoke test.

  ## Evidence

  - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
  - [x] Trace/log snippets
  - [ ] Screenshot/recording
  - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

  Local verification included:

  ```text
  ✅ Opening: http://127.0.0.1:3200/#token=<redacted>

  Also run:

  bash -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh
  zsh -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh
  pnpm build
  pnpm check

  pnpm test was attempted but hit unrelated failures in agent/provider/plugin shards before completion.

  ```

  ## Human Verification (required)

  - Verified scenarios: Shell syntax under bash and zsh; fresh zsh invocation; Docker Compose gateway port resolution; `clawdock-dashboard` opens the published host port with browser launch stubbed; token is redacted in displayed output.
  - Edge cases checked: IPv4 wildcard publish address, IPv6 wildcard publish address, URL encoding for tokens, preserving scheme/path during
  port remap, config-token precedence over project `.env` token, avoiding zsh `path` variable shadowing, avoiding `head`/`tr`/`grep` dependencies on the dashboard path.
  - What you did **not** verify: Full clean `pnpm test`; it was attempted but failed in unrelated shards before completion.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: Token handling in the shell helper could expose the dashboard token in terminal logs.
    - Mitigation: The browser/open command receives the tokenized URL, but displayed and fallback output use `#token=<redacted>`.

  - Risk: Remapping could break non-root dashboard paths or TLS.
    - Mitigation: The helper preserves the original URL scheme and path while replacing only the host/port authority.

  - Risk: Shell helper behavior may vary between bash and zsh or minimal PATH environments.
    - Mitigation: The dashboard path avoids `head`, `tr`, and `grep`, avoids zsh’s special `path` variable, and was smoke-tested from a fresh zsh invocation.
target.author: dhoman

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
